goal: Complete the `ucil plugin` CLI command tree by landing the five remaining subcommands (`list`, `uninstall`, `enable`, `disable`, `reload`) on top of the existing `install` (WO-0013) so that the full set documented in master-plan Â§16 line 1580 (`plugin list | install <n> | uninstall <n> | enable <n> | disable <n> | reload`) works end-to-end. Every subcommand MUST support `--format json` and `--format text` output (matching the existing `install` precedent in `crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs:33-46`). Each subcommand has a frozen-selector unit/integration test under `commands::plugin::*` so the feature-list selector `cargo test -p ucil-cli commands::plugin::` (P2-W6-F07 acceptance) runs every new test. The WO additionally lands the deferred `pub use` re-exports for `CapabilitiesSection`, `ActivationSection`, `ResourcesSection` in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` (WO-0042 carry-forward, lessons-learned line 82) so the CLI can introspect plugin capabilities without reaching into private daemon modules.
plan_summary: Five new subcommand variants on `PluginSubcommand` (`crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs`), each with its own `Args` struct, handler async function, and test. Persistence of per-plugin `enabled` / `installed` state lives in a single TOML state file (path chosen by the executor; recommended `<plugins_dir>/.ucil-plugin-state.toml` so it sits next to the manifests it tracks). `list` enumerates discovered manifests + their state. `install` keeps current probe+report semantics (existing test stays green) AND additionally writes `installed=true` to the state file. `uninstall` writes `installed=false`. `enable` / `disable` toggle `enabled`. `reload` re-runs the existing health-check probe (no daemon IPC channel exists in Phase 2; reload is in-process, parity with the existing `install` flow). All five new subcommands honour `--format json` with the same `serde_json::to_writer_pretty` shape used by `install`. The deferred `pub use` for `CapabilitiesSection`, `ActivationSection`, `ResourcesSection` in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` lands as a single 3-line additive edit. NO modification to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs` (tightening `PluginManager::add` to return `Result<(), AlreadyRegistered | LockContention>` is technical debt deferred to a future WO with an ADR â€” F07 has no register-into-running-daemon surface that would consume the new return type).
scope_in: crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs â€” EDIT. Extend `PluginSubcommand` enum with five new variants (`List(ListArgs)`, `Uninstall(UninstallArgs)`, `Enable(EnableArgs)`, `Disable(DisableArgs)`, `Reload(ReloadArgs)`). Each new `*Args` struct mirrors `InstallArgs`'s shape (`plugins_dir: PathBuf`, `format: OutputFormat`, plus a positional `name` for the four name-targeted subcommands). The dispatcher `run_with_writer` matches the new variants and routes each to a handler async function (`list_plugins`, `uninstall_plugin`, `enable_plugin`, `disable_plugin`, `reload_plugin`). EVERY handler returns a `Result<*Outcome, PluginCmdError>` (extend the existing enum if a new domain variant is needed; e.g. `PluginCmdError::StateRead { path, source }` for state-file IO failures). EVERY handler emits its outcome through `emit_*` helpers parameterised on `OutputFormat`, matching the existing `emit_text` / `emit_json` precedent for `install`. JSON output for each subcommand is a single newline-terminated JSON object whose shape is documented in a `# JSON output` section of each `*Args` rustdoc (executor picks the field set; minimum: `name` + `status` + a subcommand-specific payload).

crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs â€” ADD: per-plugin state persistence. Define a private struct (`PluginStateEntry { name: String, enabled: bool, installed: bool }`) and a single TOML state file (recommended path: `<plugins_dir>/.ucil-plugin-state.toml`) read/written through two private helpers (`read_state(plugins_dir: &Path) -> Result<Vec<PluginStateEntry>, PluginCmdError>` and `write_state(plugins_dir: &Path, entries: &[PluginStateEntry]) -> Result<(), PluginCmdError>`). Both helpers are crash-safe via tempfile-then-rename (`tempfile::NamedTempFile::persist_noclobber` or `tokio::fs::write` to a sibling tmp + `tokio::fs::rename`). State file is created lazily on first mutation; `read_state` returns `Ok(vec![])` when the file is absent. `install` and `reload` write `installed=true`; `uninstall` writes `installed=false`; `enable`/`disable` toggle `enabled`. `list` reads state + walks plugins_dir manifests and joins them by `name` (a discovered manifest with no state entry defaults to `installed=false, enabled=false`).

crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs â€” ADD: five new module-root acceptance tests (sibling-style with the existing `test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name` at line 381), each `#[tokio::test]`, each named with `commands::plugin::test_<subcommand>_*` so the F07 frozen selector `cargo test -p ucil-cli commands::plugin::` matches all of them: (1) `test_plugin_list_returns_all_discovered_manifests` â€” TempDir with two `plugin.toml` files (`alpha`, `beta`), runs `--format json`, asserts the array contains both names with their resolved `enabled`/`installed` defaults. (2) `test_plugin_uninstall_marks_state_file` â€” pre-creates a state file with `installed=true`, runs `uninstall alpha`, re-reads state and asserts `installed=false`. (3) `test_plugin_enable_marks_state_file` â€” runs `enable alpha`, re-reads state and asserts `enabled=true`. (4) `test_plugin_disable_marks_state_file` â€” runs `disable alpha`, asserts `enabled=false`. (5) `test_plugin_reload_runs_health_check` â€” TempDir with `plugin.toml` pointing at the real `mock-mcp-plugin` binary (same setup as `test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name`), runs `reload alpha --format json`, asserts the response carries `tool_count >= 1` AND state file shows `installed=true`. NO mocks of `tokio::process::Command`. Tests at module root (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) for selector-stability, matching the existing `test_plugin_install_*` placement and DEC-0007.

crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs â€” ADD additional unit tests INSIDE the existing `mod tests { ... }` block (lines 457-553) for narrow happy-path coverage: `list_emits_json_array`, `uninstall_emits_status_field`, `enable_emits_status_field`, `disable_emits_status_field`, `reload_emits_tool_count_field`. Five `#[test]` (not `#[tokio::test]`) functions exercising the `emit_*` helpers directly with stub outcome structs; mirror `emit_text_contains_plugin_name_and_tool_list` and `emit_json_has_tool_count_and_status_fields` (lines 524-552).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs â€” EDIT: extend the `pub use plugin_manager::{ ... }` block (lines 105-109) by adding `ActivationSection`, `CapabilitiesSection`, `ResourcesSection` alphabetically into the existing 3-line list. Single additive edit; the new types are already `pub struct` in `plugin_manager.rs:165, 187, 207` (verified before WO emission) so no source changes to plugin_manager are required. WO-0042 lessons-learned line 82 explicitly names this WO as the one that lands these re-exports. NO other lib.rs change.

scripts/verify/P2-W6-F07.sh â€” NEW. The script (a) `set -euo pipefail`, (b) `cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"`, (c) `cargo build -p ucil-cli --quiet 2>&1 | tee /tmp/wo-0045-build.log`, (d) creates a TempDir-based plugins fixture with TWO `plugin.toml` files (e.g. `tmp/plugins/alpha/plugin.toml` + `tmp/plugins/beta/plugin.toml`, both pointing at the `mock-mcp-plugin` binary) so `list` has something non-trivial to enumerate, (e) runs each subcommand end-to-end via `target/debug/ucil plugin <subcommand> [name] --plugins-dir <tmp> --format json` and asserts the JSON shape via `jq -e` (e.g. `jq -e '.plugins | length == 2' < /tmp/wo-0045-list.json`, `jq -e '.installed == false' < /tmp/wo-0045-uninstall.json`, etc.), (f) verifies the state file at `<tmp>/.ucil-plugin-state.toml` reflects the cumulative mutations, (g) runs `cargo test -p ucil-cli commands::plugin:: 2>&1 | tee /tmp/wo-0045-cargo.log` and `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. [0-9]+ passed; 0 failed|[0-9]+ tests run: [0-9]+ passed' /tmp/wo-0045-cargo.log` (alternation regex per WO-0038/0039/0042/0043/0044 lesson), (h) on green prints `[OK] P2-W6-F07` and exits 0; on any failure prints `[FAIL] P2-W6-F07: <reason>` and exits 1. `chmod +x` so it's executable.

Commit ladder â€” approximately 7 commits, each â‰¤50 LOC except the test files (DEC-0005 module-coherence applies for the test commit): (1) `feat(daemon): pub use CapabilitiesSection/ActivationSection/ResourcesSection in lib.rs` (~3 LOC), (2) `feat(cli): add PluginStateEntry + read_state/write_state helpers in commands/plugin.rs` (~50 LOC), (3) `feat(cli): add `plugin list` subcommand with --format json` (~40 LOC), (4) `feat(cli): add `plugin uninstall|enable|disable` state-mutating subcommands` (~50 LOC), (5) `feat(cli): add `plugin reload` subcommand wrapping health_check_with_timeout` (~40 LOC), (6) `test(cli): add commands::plugin::test_<subcommand>_* module-root tests for list/uninstall/enable/disable/reload` (~150 LOC; over budget per DEC-0005 module-coherence â€” single coherent test file additions land together), (7) `feat(verify): implement scripts/verify/P2-W6-F07.sh end-to-end` (~80 LOC bash).
scope_out: NO modification to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs`. Tightening `PluginManager::add` from silent contention fall-through to `Result<(), PluginError::AlreadyRegistered | PluginError::LockContention>` (WO-0043 lessons-learned line 121, technical debt entry line 139) is DEFERRED to a future WO that introduces actual CLI â†” running-daemon IPC. F07's CLI subcommands all operate on disk state + spawn-and-probe; none of them call `PluginManager::add`, so the tightening has no consumer in scope. Document the deferral in this WO's ready-for-review note.

NO modification to ANY plugin manifest under `plugins/**`. Frozen by WO-0044; `forbidden_paths` blanket-bans `plugins/structural/**` and `plugins/search/**`. The new tests use TempDir-fixture manifests, not the real ast-grep / probe / serena manifests.

NO modification to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**` other than the single `pub use` line addition to `lib.rs`. The plugin parser (WO-0042), lifecycle / hot-reload / circuit-breaker code (WO-0042/0043), and integration test bindings (WO-0044) are FROZEN.

NO modification to `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` or `crates/ucil-cli/Cargo.toml`. The CLI already depends on `ucil-daemon`, `walkdir`, `tempfile`, `tokio`, `serde_json`, `thiserror`, `clap`. The new state-file persistence reuses `toml` (already a `ucil-cli` workspace dep at line 21) for serialisation.
NO new crate, NO new workspace member.
NO new daemon binary, NO new daemon entry point, NO MCP server changes.
NO lifecycle integration test suite (`tests/integration/test_plugin_lifecycle.rs`) â€” that is feature P2-W6-F08 in a sibling WO and depends on the F07 CLI surface (this WO).
NO G1 fusion logic, NO G2 RRF, NO `find_references` / `search_code` MCP tool wiring â€” those are P2-W7-F01..F09.
NO embedding pipeline, NO ONNX, NO LanceDB, NO CodeRankEmbed â€” those are P2-W8-F01..F08.
NO modification to `tests/fixtures/**` â€” fixtures are frozen by every WO. The new TempDir fixtures live entirely in test-time tempdirs.
NO `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `xfail`, commented-out assertions, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies â€” forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.

NO mock of `tokio::process::Command`, `Child`, `ChildStdio`, or any other process-spawning surface. The `reload` subcommand spawns the real `mock-mcp-plugin` binary in tests, identical to the existing `test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name` precedent.
NO `git commit --no-verify`, `--amend` after push, or force-push.
acceptance: `PluginSubcommand` enum has six variants (`Install`, `List`, `Uninstall`, `Enable`, `Disable`, `Reload`) â€” visible in `crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs` (P2-W6-F07 deliverable, master-plan Â§16 line 1580).

`ucil plugin list --plugins-dir <dir> --format json` enumerates every `plugin.toml` under `<dir>` (max-depth 3, matching the existing `install` walker) and emits a JSON array; each element carries at least `name`, `enabled` (bool), `installed` (bool).
`ucil plugin uninstall <name> --plugins-dir <dir> --format json` writes `installed=false` for `<name>` to the state file; subsequent `list` shows the change.
`ucil plugin enable <name> --plugins-dir <dir> --format json` writes `enabled=true`.
`ucil plugin disable <name> --plugins-dir <dir> --format json` writes `enabled=false`.

`ucil plugin reload <name> --plugins-dir <dir> --format json` re-runs `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout` against the named manifest, asserts at least one tool, and persists `installed=true`. Output JSON carries `tool_count >= 1`.

State file at `<plugins_dir>/.ucil-plugin-state.toml` is crash-safe: writes go through tempfile-then-rename (single `tokio::fs::rename` atomically replaces). Concurrent reads from `list` while a `uninstall` is in flight either see the old state OR the new state â€” never a torn read.

`crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` has `pub use plugin_manager::{... ActivationSection, CapabilitiesSection, ... ResourcesSection ...}` after the WO lands (WO-0042 deferred work, lessons-learned line 82).

Five new module-root tests are present and named `commands::plugin::test_plugin_list_returns_all_discovered_manifests`, `commands::plugin::test_plugin_uninstall_marks_state_file`, `commands::plugin::test_plugin_enable_marks_state_file`, `commands::plugin::test_plugin_disable_marks_state_file`, `commands::plugin::test_plugin_reload_runs_health_check`. Each runs against a TempDir fixture with NO mocks of subprocess spawning.
`cargo test -p ucil-cli commands::plugin::` (P2-W6-F07 frozen selector) runs ALL new + existing `commands::plugin::*` tests and exits 0.
`cargo test -p ucil-cli commands::plugin::test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name` still passes (P1-W5-F01 / WO-0013 regression guard â€” existing `install` semantics unchanged).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manager` still passes (P2-W6-F01 / WO-0009 regression guard).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_manifest_parser` still passes (P2-W6-F01 regression guard).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_lifecycle_state_machine` still passes (P2-W6-F02 regression guard).

`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test plugin_manifests` still passes (P2-W6-F05 + P2-W6-F06 regression guard from WO-0044). Both `plugin_manifests::ast_grep_manifest_health_check` and `plugin_manifests::probe_manifest_health_check` stay green; the `pub use` additions in `lib.rs` are additive and cannot break the existing import set.
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_hot_cold_lifecycle` still passes (P1-W3-F06 regression guard).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_stdio` still passes (Phase-1 stub-path regression guard).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_with_kg` still passes (Phase-1 KG-bootstrap regression guard).
`cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` passes â€” no regression in any other crate.

`cargo clippy -p ucil-cli --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean (test files lint-clean, including `clippy::pedantic` defaults). NEW handlers MUST avoid the lints flagged on WO-0043: `clippy::significant_drop_tightening` (consume guards inline at the expression boundary), `clippy::single_match_else` (use `if .is_ok()` shape), `clippy::doc_markdown` (every `MAX_RESTARTS`-style identifier in doc comments wrapped in backticks).

`shellcheck scripts/verify/P2-W6-F07.sh` clean (or absent â€” if shellcheck is not on PATH, skip with a clear log line).

Frozen-selector placement (DEC-0007 + WO-0042/0043/0044 lesson): the five new acceptance tests land at module-root in `crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs` (matching the existing `test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name` at line 381), NOT inside `mod tests {}`. The narrow unit tests (5 emit-helper coverage) go inside `mod tests {}` matching the existing `emit_text_contains_*` / `emit_json_has_*` precedent.

Backward-compat regression guards: `acceptance_criteria` runs every prior WO's frozen selector explicitly (`test_manifest_parser`, `test_lifecycle_state_machine`, `test_hot_reload`, `test_circuit_breaker`, `test_hot_cold_lifecycle`, `--test plugin_manager`, `--test plugin_manifests`, `--test e2e_mcp_stdio`, `--test e2e_mcp_with_kg`, `test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name`).

Single-source-of-edit blast radius (extended): the WO touches exactly THREE files: `crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs` (subcommand handlers + tests), `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` (3 `pub use` lines), and `scripts/verify/P2-W6-F07.sh` (new). `forbidden_paths` blanket-bans every other crate src + every other plugin manifest + every other crate Cargo.toml so the executor cannot accidentally widen scope.

Cargo-test summary regex with alternation (WO-0038/0039/0042/0043/0044 lesson): every `acceptance_criteria` cargo-test entry uses `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. ... 0 failed|... tests run: ... passed'`.

No-mocks-of-critical-deps (root CLAUDE.md): the `reload` subcommand spawns the real `mock-mcp-plugin` binary in tests, identical to the existing `test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name` precedent. NO `tokio::process::Command` mocking. The state-file persistence tests use real `tempfile::TempDir` + real `tokio::fs::write` / `tokio::fs::rename` â€” no in-memory FS shim.

Clippy lint pre-emption (WO-0043 lessons line 125-128): every new handler avoids `clippy::significant_drop_tightening` (consume `tokio::sync::RwLock` guards inline at the expression boundary), `clippy::single_match_else` (use `if .is_ok()` shape), `clippy::doc_markdown` (every uppercase identifier in doc comments wrapped in backticks). Pre-flight grep `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs` before pushing.

DEC-0005 module-coherence-commits: the bundled new tests commit (~150 LOC for five module-root acceptance tests) is one commit per file even if over the ~50 LOC soft target. Documented in scope_in commit ladder commit 6.
Operator-actionable failure messages in verify scripts: `scripts/verify/P2-W6-F07.sh` uses `[OK] P2-W6-F07` / `[FAIL] P2-W6-F07: <reason>` exit semantics matching WO-0042/0043/0044 convention.

Phase-log Phase-2 standing rules item 1 (stdout pristine on stdio MCP path) â€” N/A directly here since the CLI is a different code path; the new subcommands write to `std::io::stdout()` via the existing `run_with_writer` injection, and tests capture into `Vec<u8>` without touching the daemon's stdio MCP server.

Phase-log Phase-2 standing rules item 2 (no mocks of Serena/LSP/ast-grep/Probe/etc.) â€” the `reload` subcommand spawns `mock-mcp-plugin` (same as existing `install` test); other handlers are pure state-file mutations (no subprocess). The mock binary is a UCIL-OWNED test fixture (`crates/ucil-daemon/src/bin/mock-mcp-plugin.rs`), not a mock OF a real plugin, so the no-mocks invariant is honoured.
